title: A Test Before Trying

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Cold open without context
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical but not original in this context

jokes.text: In a world, in a hemisphere, in a continent, in a country, in a state, in a city, in a neighborhood, in a street, in a house, in a living room, five heroes would be called.
jokes.setup: Epic movie trailer voiceover building to something momentous
jokes.punchline: The 'quest' is just sitting on the couch
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure, parodying action movie trailers with mundane reality

jokes.text: Let's sit on that couch.
jokes.setup: Epic buildup about heroes and dangerous quests
jokes.punchline: The 'dangerous quest' is sitting on furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax to the epic setup, brilliant economy and structure

jokes.text: And in retrospect, city officials regret holding the gang truce negotiations at Santa's Village.
jokes.setup: News report about gang negotiations
jokes.punchline: Held at children's Christmas attraction
jokes.explanation: Sharp juxtaposition of serious crime with wholesome venue

jokes.text: Apparently there are a few stairs he's struggling to climb. He's up the stairs but is now being buffeted by the breeze.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns approaching to make announcement
jokes.punchline: He's so frail he can't handle stairs or wind
jokes.explanation: Good character-based physical comedy, though predictable for Burns

jokes.text: And they've put some rocks in his pocket
jokes.setup: Burns being buffeted by breeze
jokes.punchline: Need to weigh him down like a balloon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Burns' frailty to absurd extreme

jokes.text: To decide how much your electricity bills will rise, I've assembled a team of distinguished economists and placed plausible rate hike percentages on their backs. Now we will use unfettered free market principles to arrive at a number. Release the hounds.
jokes.setup: Formal announcement about rate determination
jokes.punchline: Using dogs to chase economists for random number
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate decision-making and free market ideology through Burns' evil absurdity

jokes.text: Notice how the Keynesians climb trees while the Austrian school economists hide under rocks. That is fascinating.
jokes.setup: Dogs chasing economists with numbers
jokes.punchline: Burns treats it like nature documentary
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the economist-hunting gag with academic economics knowledge

jokes.text: Let me guess, you're going to release the hounds again. Oh, don't be silly. Release the radioactive steam.
jokes.setup: Kent predicts another hound release
jokes.punchline: Burns escalates to radioactive steam
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and escalation showing Burns' casual cruelty

jokes.text: I know it's a kid's show, but I got to see you topless.
jokes.setup: TV station cutting costs, talking to performer
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment on children's show
jokes.explanation: Typical Krusty inappropriate behavior, in-character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: This isn't your dressing room. I know, but the monkey's got a girl in my dressing room.
jokes.setup: Krusty being inappropriate in wrong room
jokes.punchline: His monkey is having sex in his actual dressing room
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that's very Krusty-show appropriate

jokes.text: Do I get a vote? That's just air escaping his lungs.
jokes.setup: Mel asking if he gets input on show cancellation
jokes.punchline: Krusty dismisses him as essentially dead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Mel's irrelevance, good character dynamics

jokes.text: Oh, I guess we'll be peeling our shrimp by hand this year.
jokes.setup: Family facing financial hardship from rate hike
jokes.punchline: Marge's idea of hardship is manual shrimp peeling
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge obliviousness to class privilege, perfectly in character

jokes.text: When I found out shrimp cocktails had no alcohol, I really lost interest.
jokes.setup: Discussion about giving up shrimp cocktails
jokes.punchline: Homer only wanted them for imaginary alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic and drinking obsession, very economical

jokes.text: Sometimes Y: the story of a vowel that goes both ways.
jokes.setup: Educational film title about the letter Y
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about vowel/consonant duality
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining grammar education with sexual double entendre

jokes.text: You said you weren't going to tell. Oh, wait, you didn't. So no one knows who the Milhouse is.
jokes.setup: Teacher accidentally reveals spy identity
jokes.punchline: Self-corrects but still reveals it's Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing teacher incompetence

jokes.text: Why did I sell one to Nelson? Hmm. Sounds better than half the kids in the band.
jokes.setup: Skinner regrets selling trombone mute to Nelson
jokes.punchline: Nelson's bullying actually improves the band
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion where Nelson's violence accidentally helps

jokes.text: I don't teach to the test. I teach 'em to dodge balls. Things they can use in life. How is that useful? Ow.
jokes.setup: Gym teacher defends teaching dodgeball as life skill
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by hitting questioner
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate physical demonstration of abstract concept

jokes.text: Who else would hire a lunch lady accused of poisoning her husband? Never convicted. They couldn't find the body.
jokes.setup: Lunch lady defending her hiring despite accusations
jokes.punchline: Gets progressively more incriminating
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor escalation, character-appropriate

jokes.text: Anyone care for some chicken-Pete pie? Did you say 'chicken-Pete pie'? His friends called him Pete.
jokes.setup: Offering what sounds like chicken pot pie
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's serving her dead husband Pete
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark pun escalation of the poisoning story

jokes.text: Dogs, cats, octopuses, octopi, octopuseseseses.
jokes.setup: Homer reading Marge's list of forbidden items
jokes.punchline: Gets confused by octopus pluralization
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer linguistic confusion, in character

jokes.text: Parking meter, you're working for me now.
jokes.setup: Homer finding items at dump
jokes.punchline: Plans to use parking meter for profit
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's parking meter scam subplot effectively

jokes.text: Mannequin heads, you're working for me now.
jokes.setup: Homer already established pattern with parking meter
jokes.punchline: Continues pattern with more absurd items
jokes.explanation: Decent running gag extension but less impactful than original

jokes.text: I hate waiting; that's why I hate risotto. Even mushroom risotto? What do you think?!
jokes.setup: Chalmers explains hating waiting via risotto analogy
jokes.punchline: Explosive reaction to Skinner's innocent question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chalmers/Skinner dynamic, explosive overreaction

jokes.text: Not as bad as the worst school in the state. Oh, thank God. You did worse than the worst school in the state, which now makes this school the worst school in the state.
jokes.setup: Seems like they avoided being worst
jokes.punchline: They're actually worse than worst
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure creating false hope then crushing it

jokes.text: Why did you say that to us in the meanest way you could? To show you that there's no way out. Except... Except what? No, I meant you should accept your terrible fate. Oh.
jokes.setup: Chalmers seems to offer hope with 'Except...'
jokes.punchline: He was just being dramatically fatalistic
jokes.explanation: Excellent double misdirection, perfectly cruel Chalmers moment

jokes.text: This student filled in everything but the ovals. In art school he'd be a genius. This isn't an art school. We tried to have ourselves accredited as one, but no one here could, uh, draw the pirate.
jokes.setup: Student failed test by missing the point entirely
jokes.punchline: School so bad they can't even draw simple pirate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing school incompetence, mysterious pirate reference

jokes.text: Sherri, you're going to West Ogdenville. Terri, North Haverbrook. But we've never been separated. I'm not sure we can even live apart.
jokes.setup: Twin sisters being separated for first time
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis over separation
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but fairly predictable twin joke

jokes.text: Stop your whinging. It's not like you're losing a rake or a backhoe.
jokes.setup: Twins upset about separation
jokes.punchline: Willie compares them to gardening equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie callousness, treating kids like tools

jokes.text: Nelson, you're off to Arkham Elementary School for the Criminally Insane.
jokes.setup: Assigning students to new schools
jokes.punchline: Nelson gets sent to psychiatric facility
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor matching Nelson's violent tendencies

jokes.text: All my extra credit points are like frequent flyer miles on a bankrupt airline.
jokes.setup: Martin upset about school closure
jokes.punchline: Compares academic achievement to worthless airline miles
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy perfectly fitting Martin's character, smart and relatable

jokes.text: Driving you crazy instead of pulling off your legs. I've really matured.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining what he was doing instead of test
jokes.punchline: His idea of maturity is psychological vs physical torture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic showing his warped sense of progress

jokes.text: Let it be Martin, let it be Martin. It's Bart. I have an aunt named Hope. Perhaps you'd like to kill her as well.
jokes.setup: Skinner hoping for good student to save them
jokes.punchline: Gets worst student, dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd dramatic metaphor, classic Skinner

jokes.text: When I think begging, I don't imagine someone standing up.
jokes.setup: Skinner begging Bart to study
jokes.punchline: Bart critiques the form of begging
jokes.explanation: Sharp Bart observation about proper begging technique

jokes.text: Well, sure the Lord wouldn't have placed his meter there if he didn't want me to park.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy justifying illegal parking
jokes.punchline: Uses religious logic for selfish act
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing CBG's self-serving rationalization

jokes.text: This is the easiest money I've ever made. Lugging around a 90-pound parking meter.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating easy money
jokes.punchline: Describes obviously difficult physical labor
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contradiction, classic Homer obliviousness

jokes.text: Oh, God, my back. Suckers. Oh, my disks are grinding. Easy money.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming this is easy work
jokes.punchline: Continues claiming it while in obvious pain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the ironic 'easy money' running gag

jokes.text: Thanks for the freedom of speech, dorks.
jokes.setup: Bart being disrespectful to founding fathers
jokes.punchline: Uses freedom to insult those who gave it to him
jokes.explanation: Smart irony about using freedom to disrespect its creators

jokes.text: Nothing a few George Washingtons won't solve.
jokes.setup: Homer facing consequences for parking scam
jokes.punchline: Plans to bribe with money featuring Washington
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism for money, fits Homer's corruption

jokes.text: I'm a kid you've never seen, and I speak for all the kids you've never seen. Do it, man, or you'll never see us again.
jokes.setup: Random background character addressing Bart
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges their own invisibility in the show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about background character status, very clever writing

jokes.text: Well, I slept on my books, so I might have gotten something through osmosis. So, you know what osmosis is? Uh, pajamas?
jokes.setup: Bart claims to know osmosis from sleeping on books
jokes.punchline: Proves he doesn't know it by guessing 'pajamas'
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Bart's ignorance despite confidence

jokes.text: Our entire school riding on that coffee can head of yours.
jokes.setup: Skinner expressing pressure on Bart
jokes.punchline: Describes Bart's head as coffee can shaped
jokes.explanation: Amusing insult that references Bart's distinctive animation

jokes.text: Don't do it, Seymour. You've never broken school rules. Ever. You wimp.
jokes.setup: Skinner debating whether to break rules
jokes.punchline: His conscience calls him a wimp for following rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner internal conflict, conscience berating his weakness

jokes.text: Well, well, well... Looks like Mr. Vanilla just grew some chocolate chips.
jokes.setup: Skinner pulled fire alarm breaking rules
jokes.punchline: Willie's ice cream metaphor for gaining edge
jokes.explanation: Creative food metaphor perfectly capturing Skinner's character shift

jokes.text: Save your analogies for the analogy portion of the exam.
jokes.setup: Willie makes ice cream analogy
jokes.punchline: Proctor treats it like a test section
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about test structure, deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Pass this test and the eggs are on me. They will be, Seymour. They will be.
jokes.setup: Skinner offering to pay for eggs if Bart passes
jokes.punchline: Bart interpreting as threat of egg-throwing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good character-specific interpretation

jokes.text: But, of course, that question mark could be an exclamation point or an interrobang.
jokes.setup: Police sketch of parking meter thief
jokes.punchline: Includes obscure punctuation terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever use of obscure grammar term, shows writers' knowledge

jokes.text: just to spite me?
jokes.setup: Lisa lecturing Bart about selfishness
jokes.punchline: Bart appears to fall asleep during her speech
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing Bart's dismissiveness

jokes.text: There's four of us and eight slices. We don't know how to divide it.
jokes.setup: Kids with leftover pizza
jokes.punchline: Can't do simple division despite being in school
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective joke about educational failure

jokes.text: Ha, I wish I was a hooker. No one in this town has any money.
jokes.setup: Being asked if she's a 'teacher of the night'
jokes.punchline: Wishes she was prostitute but town too broke
jokes.explanation: Dark humor fitting Krabappel's cynical character

jokes.text: I only know 24 letters.
jokes.setup: Bart listing his academic problems
jokes.punchline: Somehow knows wrong number of alphabet letters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart ignorance, somehow wrong about basic facts

jokes.text: I don't shut anything down. People cut their own heads, and I just tip 'em into the basket.
jokes.setup: Marge blaming proctor for closing school
jokes.punchline: Guillotine metaphor for educational execution
jokes.explanation: Darkly creative metaphor showing proctor's detachment

jokes.text: You know what they do to parking meter thieves in prison? They probably treat 'em pretty well. But when you get out, you're not eligible for jury duty. Which, again, sounds pretty good.
jokes.setup: Wiggum threatens Homer with prison consequences
jokes.punchline: All consequences sound like benefits to Homer
jokes.explanation: Good subversion where threats become selling points

jokes.text: Eat silver, copper.
jokes.setup: Wiggum shooting at Homer
jokes.punchline: Confusing metals in classic action movie line
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum confusion, plays with action movie cliches

jokes.text: Stay with me, man. Stay with me. No, no! You're the first successful business I've ever run.
jokes.setup: Homer's parking meter apparently dying
jokes.punchline: Treats inanimate object as business partner
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's attachment to objects

jokes.text: Now, what is the capital of Massachusetts? Uh, 'B'?
jokes.setup: Lisa teaching Bart test-taking strategy
jokes.punchline: Bart applies 'guess B' rule to everything
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier 'guess B' advice

jokes.text: D'oh. Will you stop that?
jokes.setup: Bart says 'D'oh' in response to multiple choice D
jokes.punchline: Homer annoyed at his catchphrase being used
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Homer's signature phrase

jokes.text: But I was blessed to receive 103. I'm returning the extra towel, unused...
jokes.setup: Homer writing complaint letter about product
jokes.punchline: Complaining about getting MORE than promised
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of typical complaint letter

jokes.text: The pillows are safe at my workstation.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about missing pillows
jokes.punchline: Homer calls dump 'workstation'
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion about his legitimate business

jokes.text: The least common blood type is A) B, B) A, C) AB, D) O... d'oh! Ten more seconds.
jokes.setup: Bart reading multiple choice question
jokes.punchline: Says 'd'oh' when seeing option D
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier D'oh moment

jokes.text: That'll do, bug, that'll do.
jokes.setup: Bart passes test by one point
jokes.punchline: Skinner quotes Babe movie to Bart
jokes.explanation: Decent movie reference fitting the moment

jokes.text: Perhaps this was a test of my humanity. A test I like to think I passed.
jokes.setup: Proctor reflecting on helping Bart
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory about basic human decency
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment about moral self-assessment

jokes.text: Would you like to have coffee or dinner, or just talk? None of the above. Testward ho!
jokes.setup: Marge asking proctor on date
jokes.punchline: Proctor rejects using test language
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with testing theme

tmdb_overview: The fate of Springfield Elementary rests on Bart and his test scores, and Homer finds way to make money with his own parking meter.
